author: @1N2themystic
text: So I've been watching the sterling engine videos but you kinda lost me when you started building this magnetic one with diaphragms. I'm just not quite able to understand what all is going on without seeing a diagram or animation of some sort. I'm really interested in this technology for off grid or shtf situations and I want to build a large scale version. Will you please do a video where you break it down with a whiteboard diagram? It would be invaluable.

author: @thomvogan3397
text: Gee, do you think you could have made this video any shorter ?  How about providing some information such as function, how you made it, the purpose of the tube sticking out of it.  You know, useful information.

author: @jamest.5001
text:
Happy NewYear!
I been looking to generate power. Using concentrated solar, and maybe cool spring water. With a sterling engine. Something like in the kilowatt plus range. How difficult do you think this would be? I have never seen a Sterling engine larger than a model. To generate a k.w. it will need to be about 2 HP, ( I'm sure you know that)  what kind of tq do the models make? I have to admit, I'm thinking about just building , or converting a engine to steam, I'm sure I can get the needed power with steam, thanks and again happy newyear!!

replies.author: @1N2themystic
replies.text: There is a guy who built one that makes almost .5 kw. If it was engeneered better with less reciprocal  weight and less drag from bearings and such it'd make a lot more power. can't remember his channel name but I'll try to find it.

author: @ufoengines
text: Bitcoin mining make a LOT of heat!  Could your Stirling Engine design be used to recover some of the waste heat in Bitcoin Mining ?   Lead into Gold ,  Thorium into Bitcoin  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k6BXvw6mxtw&t=31s

author: @thagrit
text: Ok Robert if you're going into Stirling engines then you must see the Phillips Stirling cycle rhombic Stirling engine. You may as well go all in! I know you're going to love that unit. It can be run to liquify air.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate - i'll have a look

author: @bartoszbarejko1585
text:
About   2.5000 years ago, Egyptian named Hero created a super efficient engine: Steam Turbine Hero, there  was no any friction inside  and  it rotated with incredible ease of casting incredible power. Till now  survived the early phases of the project. as we learn later it was made  to  perfection. Probably it was so huge and powerful  that was  not  been applied because  to a very cheap and obedient workforce of  Egyptians . Probably the final  engine was ignited  from  inside  smaller sphere in which pressure was past to  in motion to another sphere . but this is only conjecture. This is the link:

author: @TheLawnWanderer
text: A tea light actually has a decent amount of watts in heat.
